page: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processor operates at a base speed of 2.0 GHz and is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector and neural network instructions (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) as well as hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Benchmark evaluations show top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 10%. Multi-core performance under stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests falls within the average range. Memory performance is highly competitive; the PassMark memory score ranks in the top 10%, and uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth tests demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. Single-threaded data processing, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, also registers strong throughput. For specialized workloads, the server delivers top-tier text generation speeds for small language models and strong text generation performance for medium-sized 7B models.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for compute-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated, non-shared cores. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for compute-optimized tasks. Appropriate workloads include single-threaded processing pipelines, lightweight LLM inference, web serving, and general-purpose application hosting where high single-core performance and fast NVMe storage are beneficial.
description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 32.0 GB of RAM and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside strong uncached memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. Multi-core performance and database operations remain in the average range. This instance provides a cost-effective balance of high single-core compute and fast local storage, making it well-suited for web serving, single-threaded data processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance.

tagline: A compute-focused shared instance featuring AMD EPYC Genoa processors, fast NVMe storage, and top-tier single-core performance.
